dashboard.accessStatus: You are accessing Open Agent Hub as <2>{{role}}</2> in <0>{{org}}</0> / <1>{{workspace}}</1>
dashboard.emptyTableHint: No tool calls yet. You can invoke tools from Cursor / Claude Code using your MCP Token.

explorer.title: Memory Explorer
explorer.description: Browse/manage structured memories shared between all Agents and users in this workspace. Filterable by state, type, provenance, and project.

search.title: Memory Search
search.description: Relevance search based on keywords. Active memories under the workspace will be sorted by similarity. The production environment will connect to embedding/vector search; the current dev mode is LIKE + field weight.
search.emptyPrompt: Please enter search criteria and click Search

settings.title: Memory Settings
settings.description: Configure workspace-level memory write strategy, character limits, and snapshot behavior. Settings are stored in Workspace metadata.

timeline.title: Memory Timeline
timeline.description: Displays the timeline of memory-related events, sourced from audit logs where the action starts with 'memory'.

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: Skills are reusable 'procedural memories' for agents, such as fixed project commands, common script snippets, and debugging processes. States can be set to active, stale, or archived.
skills.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this skill?

form.reason: Reason (optional, will be written to audit log)
form.reasonPlaceholder: e.g., Replaced by a new process

publicSkills.title: Public Skills
publicSkills.description: Public Skills are platform-provided reusable skills. Once installed, they become available to the current workspace or selected project and are synced to .openagent/skills/ by hub.sync_project.
publicSkills.allScopesInstalled: This public skill is already installed in the workspace and all projects. Archive an install scope before reinstalling it.

form.slugRule: Use lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only
form.name: Name
form.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe when this public skill applies
form.contentPlaceholder: Reusable workflow, checklist, or operating steps for agents
form.versionHint: Changing Skill content creates a new version. Installed workspaces or projects keep their snapshot until upgraded manually.

vectorIndex.title: Vector Index Status
vectorIndex.description: Displays the embedding service status and vector index statistics.

clients.description: The Gateway automatically identifies and registers clients (Cursor / Claude Code / Windsurf / VSCode etc.) based on the User-Agent of MCP calls.

profile.title: Agent Profile (via MCP Call)
profile.description: This page directly invokes the hub.get_agent_profile tool via the MCP Gateway, demonstrating the profile details (including organization, workspace, current user, capabilities, and quotas) retrieved by the agent when using the Hub.

login.registerSuccess: Registration successful. Your personal workspace is ready to use.
login.subtitle: MCP-based AgentOps SaaS Console
login.usernamePlaceholder: Username (letters, digits, Chinese; 2-32 chars; no leading digit)
login.usernameInvalid: Username must be 2-32 chars, start with a letter or Chinese character, and contain only letters, digits, and Chinese characters
login.demoAccountHint: System has built-in demo account: Default admin username /
login.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 6 characters

workspaces.title: Workspace Management
workspaces.desc: Workspaces are tenant-level isolation units. Each workspace has independent memory, rules, tokens, tool policies, and quotas.
workspaces.name: Name
workspaces.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this team?
workspaces.deleteWarn: Deleting the team removes its rules, memory and members. You will return to the no-team state.
workspaces.leaveWarn: After leaving you return to the no-team state, and can create or join a team again.

onboarding.title: Welcome, {{name}}
onboarding.subtitle: You don't have a workspace yet. Create your own workspace, or accept an invitation to join a team.
onboarding.createDesc: After creating it you own your workspace; invite members later to turn it into a team.

members.title: Member Management
members.desc: Add members to the current workspace. Roles: owner / admin / member / viewer.
members.confirmRemove: Are you sure you want to remove this member?
members.alreadyInvited: This user has been invited and is waiting for acceptance
members.personalNoMembers: Personal workspace does not require member management

projects.title: Project Management
projects.desc: Projects are used to further isolate context under a workspace. Both Project Rules and Project Memory are bound to the project level.
projects.name: Name
projects.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this?
projects.gitRemoteHint: The repo's git remote URL — the most reliable way to identify the same project across machines/clones; resolved ahead of local path matching.
projects.projectDirHint: The project root's directory name (no path); usually identical across machines/OSes. Used to identify the same project when there is no Git remote.

detail.bindingHint: These keys identify the same project across machines and clients. Git remote is the most reliable; repo directory is a fallback.
detail.aggregateHint: Project Memory and Skills are managed in the Memory Hub, scoped to this project.
detail.syncDesc: Latest snapshot sync per member × client × machine. Clients whose ETag differs from the current bundle are flagged as outdated.
detail.noSync: No sync records yet — no agent has synced this project.

tokens.title: MCP Tokens (API Keys)
tokens.alertMsg: MCP Tokens are used by Agent clients (Cursor / Claude Code, etc.) to invoke the 14 Hub tools through the MCP Gateway.
tokens.alertDesc: The token is only displayed once upon creation. Please save it securely. The backend only stores its hash and cannot recover it.
tokens.name: Name
tokens.saveTokenAlert: Please copy and save it securely now! This token will only be shown once.
tokens.usageInstructionsPrefix: Usage: Put this token as Bearer in Authorization header, and send JSON-RPC 2.0 request to
tokens.workspaceBind: MCP Tokens are bound to the workspace at creation time ({{name}}). Switch workspaces and create separate tokens for each.

audit.title: Audit Logs
audit.desc: All critical actions from both the console and the MCP Gateway are recorded in the audit logs.

guide.title: User Guide · Getting Started
guide.descPrefix: A centralized platform for AI Agents (Cursor, Claude Code, Cline, etc.) to manage
guide.descSuffix: globally. Once connected via the MCP protocol, Agents can reuse the same settings and memories across any client.
guide.threeSteps: Three Steps to Connect Your Agent
guide.step1Desc2: page to generate a personal access token starting with
guide.step1Desc3: . It represents your identity and workspace, and is used by the Agent for authentication.
guide.step2Desc1: Write the configuration below into the corresponding client's MCP configuration file, replacing
guide.step2Desc2: with the token created in the previous step. MCP gateway address:
guide.otherDesc4: . Older clients can use legacy SSE:
guide.step3Desc1: After restarting the client, you should see the
guide.step3Desc2: tools. You can also verify directly using curl with
guide.step3Success1: If the tool list is returned, the connection is successful. You can also debug online on the
guide.projectSyncAlertTitle: Make console settings visible to Agents
guide.projectSyncAlertDesc: The console is the source of truth; local .openagent/ is a read-only snapshot. After saving rules, personal instructions, or Skills, sync them into the project root so developer Agents can read them through AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md.
guide.projectSyncMcpDesc2: at task start. The tool returns files, managed_block, and etag; the client or Agent must write the returned files into the project directory.
guide.projectSyncMcpNote: Within the same MCP session, the project binding remains active, so later project-scoped memory and rule calls inherit it automatically.
guide.whatCanDo: What You Can Do After Connecting
guide.rulesDesc3: , and they will take effect for all Agents automatically (supporting Global -> Project -> Agent override).
guide.personalDesc1: Configure default language, response style, and custom instructions in
guide.personalDesc2: ; after sync they are written to
guide.memoryDesc2: , which can be reviewed and managed in
guide.memoryDesc3: , and recalled even when switching clients.
guide.skillsDesc2: ; after sync they are written to
guide.toolsDesc2: , with credentials stored securely under encryption and protected by circuit breakers.
guide.policyDesc1: Set allow/deny lists, call limits, and second confirmation in
guide.policyDesc2: . All invocations leave footprints in the
guide.quickReference: Quick Reference for Common MCP Tools
guide.rule1Desc: Get workspace global rules (parsed and overridden by target agent)
guide.rule2Desc: Get project rules in effect (merged from global -> project -> agent)
guide.rule3Desc: Pull project rules, preferences, and relevant memory all at once
guide.mem1Desc: Retrieve memories by keyword (supports English and Chinese)
guide.mem2Desc: Automatically recall relevant memories based on current context
guide.mem3Desc: Submit memory candidate (scored by discipline, may enter pending review)
guide.conn1Desc: List connected external MCP Server tools
guide.conn2Desc: Invoke external tools verified by policies with circuit-breaker protection

context.globalRulesDesc: Workspace-wide global rules that apply to all projects and all Agents. The type is fixed to global_rule. Leaving the Agent field blank means it applies to all Agents. Agent-specific rules can still be distinguished in the Global Rules list by the agent_name field.
context.outputPreferencesDesc: Preferences that affect the output format / style / level of detail of the Agent. The type is fixed to output_preference. Leaving the Agent field blank means it applies to all Agents.

personal.title: Personal Instructions
personal.description: These settings apply only to the current user and are exposed to Agents through MCP and .openagent/local/profile.md.
personal.priorityHint: Workspace policy, tool policy, global rules, and project rules take priority over personal instructions.
personal.agentApplyDesc: After saving, ask the Agent to call hub.sync_project. Agents read the latest snapshot through AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md.
personal.agentApplyPath: The sync result is written to .openagent/local/profile.md. When others update shared rules or Skills, call hub.sync_project again to pull the new version.
personal.customPlaceholder: Example: Reply in Chinese by default; keep code, commands, paths, API fields, and error messages in their original language.

context.projectRulesDesc: Project-level rules that take effect for all Agents within that project. The type is fixed to project_rule. Switch the project above to manage rules for different projects.
context.snapshotVersionsDesc: Memory snapshots are used to freeze the workspace memory state at a certain moment, facilitating rollback or auditing. This page is a placeholder implementation and will be connected after the Snapshot API is launched.
context.workspacePolicyDesc: Workspace-level policies that affect all Agent behaviors. The type is fixed to workspace_policy. It is recommended to fill in structured JSON (e.g., security, audit, retention policies).

connectedServers.title: Connected MCP Servers
connectedServers.subtitle: Register downstream MCP services (Streamable HTTP / SSE / stdio) callable by the backend. Once configured, they can be tracked in tool policies and invocation logs.

mcpInspector.title: MCP Inspector (JSON-RPC Debugger)
mcpInspector.alertMsg: Send JSON-RPC 2.0 requests directly via POST /mcp. Default to current logged-in user's JWT; to simulate a real agent, paste a complete MCP Token (pat_xxx).
mcpInspector.errNoToken: Not logged in and no PAT provided, cannot send

toolCatalog.title: Tool Catalog
toolCatalog.subtitle: List all available tools, including Hub built-in tools (hub.*) and tools exposed by connected MCP Servers.

toolInvocations.title: Tool Invocation Logs
toolInvocations.subtitle: Record each tool called by the Agent via MCP, including parameter summary, output summary, status, latency, and confirmation requirements.

toolPolicies.title: Tool Policies
toolPolicies.subtitle: Configure for each (MCP Server, tool_name) combination: allowed, requires confirmation, daily limit, per-user limit, and risk level.

usage.title: Usage Monitoring
usage.subtitle: Tool calls and memory writes in this workspace, displayed by today, this month, active session, and other dimensions.
